steps.text: The function $f(x) = 39$ is a constant linear function. A constant function outputs the same value for every input $x$, regardless of what $x$ is.
Since $f(x) = 39$ for all $x$, we need a table where every $f(x)$ entry equals $39$, no matter which $x$ values are chosen.

Evaluating each choice: Choice A shows all outputs equal $0$, so that represents $f(x) = 0$. Choice C shows outputs $0, 39, 78$, which is $f(x) = 39x$. Choice D shows outputs $39, 0, -39$, which is $f(x) = 39 - 39x$.
Choice B shows $f(0) = 39$, $f(1) = 39$, $f(2) = 39$ — every output is $39$, matching $f(x) = 39$ exactly. That matches choice B — B is correct.

choices.text: • All three outputs are $0$, so this table represents $f(x) = 0$, not $f(x) = 39$. Eliminated.
• All three outputs are $39$, exactly matching the constant function $f(x) = 39$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
• Outputs are $0, 39, 78$ — each increases by $39$, so this represents $f(x) = 39x$, not a constant. Eliminated.
• Outputs are $39, 0, -39$ — each decreases by $39$, so this represents $f(x) = 39 - 39x$. Eliminated.
